title: Topic, Headers, Alternate, and Exchange-to-Exchange Routing · Boundary Failure Triage
summary: An incident engineer receives a plausible but defective multi-strategy routing and unroutable-message table; preserve message identity and timing, locate the first broken ownership boundary, and retain a deterministic regression case. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in multi-strategy routing and unroutable-message table and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging,…
objectives: Design bounded dot-separated topic taxonomies whose stable semantic segments support exact, star, and hash matching.
Enumerate overlapping topic bindings and prove which queues receive one copy even when multiple bindings on the same queue match.
Use headers exchanges only with typed bounded metadata and explicit all-versus-any matching, not as a schema-free routing escape hatch.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Inspect the failure record · Debug Topic Segment Design
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug messaging team handles case 5671 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to classify primary, alternate, returned, and invalid routing outcomes; allow exactly one terminal acknowledgement decision owner; then lose a quorum member and recompute majority availability. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Design bounded dot-separated topic taxonomies whose stable semantic segments support exact, star, and hash matching.

steps.why: Retrieving Exchange Chain Cycle through the concrete multi-strategy routing and unroutable-message table case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For RabbitMQ and TypeScript Publish the incident record · Debug Exchange Chain Cycle, keep Exchange Chain Cycle connected to executable TypeScript messaging decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use exchange-to-exchange bindings with bounded graph analysis, route evidence, permission checks, and cycle-safe topology review.

hints: For RabbitMQ and TypeScript Publish the incident record · Debug Exchange Chain Cycle, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Exchange Chain Cycle precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At RabbitMQ and TypeScript Publish the incident record · Debug Exchange Chain Cycle, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A debug messaging team handles case 1246 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to classify primary, alternate, returned, and invalid routing outcomes; allow exactly one terminal acknowledgement decision owner; then close the channel while one acknowledgement decision is still pending. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Use exchange-to-exchange bindings with bounded graph analysis, route evidence, permission checks, and cycle-safe topology review.

For RabbitMQ and TypeScript Publish the incident record · Debug Exchange Chain Cycle, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Exchange chains cannot create duplicate or cyclic routing behavior.
